title: 全球都在加关税，中国会怎么应对？

youtube.publish_title: Global Tariffs and China's Response | 全球加关税，中国怎么应对？

chapters.title_en: The Impact on the World's Factory
chapters.title: 对“世界工厂”的影响
应对方法一：寻找新市场

chapters.title_en: Strategy 2 & 3: Going Global and Better Quality
chapters.title: 应对方法二与三：走出去与提高质量

in_this_episode_you_will_learn: Understand what 'tariffs' are using simple everyday examples in HSK3 Chinese.
Learn why countries are raising taxes on imported goods to protect local jobs.
Discover the three main strategies Chinese businesses use to adapt to a changing global economy.
Practice your Chinese listening skills with a relaxed, slow-paced conversation.
description_intro: Welcome to our Chinese learning podcast! Today, we are talking about a very popular topic in international news: global tariffs. What is a tariff, and why are so many countries increasing them? Most importantly, how is China, the 'World's Factory', responding to these big changes?

We will break down this complex economic topic into simple, easy-to-understand Chinese suitable for HSK3 learners. Sit back, relax, and improve your Chinese listening while learning about the global economy!

blocks.purpose: 开场打招呼，用贴近生活的新闻话题引入“关税”

segments.speaker: female
segments.text: 大家好，欢迎收听我们的中文播客。我是盼盼。
segments.en: Hello everyone, welcome to our Chinese podcast. I'm Panpan.

segments.speaker: male
segments.text: 大家好，我是老路。欢迎大家和我们一起慢慢学中文。
segments.en: Hello everyone, I'm Laolu. Welcome to learn Chinese slowly with us.

segments.speaker: female
segments.text: 路哥，最近我看新闻，经常看到一个词，叫“关税”。你注意到了吗？
segments.en: Brother Lu, lately when I watch the news, I often see a word, 'tariffs'. Have you noticed?

segments.speaker: male
segments.text: 嗯，是的。最近这真的是一个非常热门的话题，全球很多国家都在讨论这个事情。
segments.en: Yes, indeed. This is really a very hot topic recently, and many countries around the world are discussing it.

segments.speaker: female
segments.text: 对啊。我有一个外国朋友，他以前经常从中国买东西卖到他的国家。但他最近经常叹气，说生意不好做了。
segments.en: Right. I have a foreign friend who used to buy things from China and sell them in his country. But he sighs a lot lately, saying business is getting hard.

segments.speaker: male
segments.text: 哎呀，这很容易理解。因为关税增加了，买东西的成本就变高了嘛。
segments.en: Ah, that's easy to understand. Because tariffs have increased, the cost of buying things has gone up.

segments.speaker: female
segments.text: 可是对于学习中文的朋友来说，可能“关税”这个词有点儿难。路哥，你能用最简单的话给大家解释一下吗？
segments.en: But for our friends learning Chinese, the word 'tariff' might be a bit difficult. Brother Lu, can you explain it to everyone in the simplest words?

blocks.purpose: 用生活中小物品的例子，拆解和解释关税的概念

segments.speaker: male
segments.text: 没问题。其实关税，就像是一种“过路费”或者“门票”。
segments.en: No problem. Actually, a tariff is like a 'toll fee' or a 'ticket'.

segments.speaker: female
segments.text: 门票？去哪里要买门票呢？难道是去公园吗？哈哈。
segments.en: A ticket? Where do you need to buy a ticket to go? Is it to the park? Haha.

segments.speaker: male
segments.text: 哈哈，不是去公园，是去别的国家的市场。比如，你在中国做了一个杯子，想卖到A国去。
segments.en: Haha, not to the park, but to another country's market. For example, you make a cup in China and want to sell it to Country A.

segments.speaker: male
segments.text: 这个杯子原来卖十块钱。但是A国说，想把杯子带进我们国家，必须交两块钱的税。这就叫关税。
segments.en: This cup originally sells for ten yuan. But Country A says, to bring the cup into our country, you must pay a two-yuan tax. This is called a tariff.

segments.speaker: female
segments.text: 哦，我明白了！那加上这两块钱，这个杯子在A国就要卖十二块钱了，对吧？
segments.en: Oh, I understand! Then adding these two yuan, this cup will have to sell for twelve yuan in Country A, right?

segments.speaker: male
segments.text: 没错。如果这时候，A国突然说，我们要增加关税，现在要收五块钱了。杯子就变得更贵了。
segments.en: Exactly. If at this time, Country A suddenly says, we are increasing the tariff, now we are collecting five yuan. The cup becomes even more expensive.

segments.speaker: female
segments.text: 哎呀，那就卖十五块钱了！如果太贵，A国的人可能就不想买这个杯子了。
segments.en: Oh my, then it will sell for fifteen yuan! If it's too expensive, people in Country A might not want to buy this cup.

segments.speaker: male
segments.text: 是的。所以你那个做生意的朋友，才会觉得越来越难做。
segments.en: Yes. That's why your friend who does business feels it's getting harder and harder.

blocks.purpose: 讨论国家提高关税背后的原因（保护本土产业和就业）

segments.speaker: female
segments.text: 可是，路哥，为什么现在很多国家都要加关税呢？大家一起轻轻松松地做生意，不好吗？
segments.en: But, Brother Lu, why are many countries raising tariffs now? Isn't it good for everyone to do business easily together?

segments.speaker: male
segments.text: 嗯，这是一个很好的问题。其实，很多国家增加关税，主要是为了“保护自己”。
segments.en: Hmm, that's a very good question. Actually, many countries increase tariffs mainly to 'protect themselves'.

segments.speaker: female
segments.text: 保护自己？是保护自己国家的公司和工人，对吗？
segments.en: Protect themselves? You mean protecting their own country's companies and workers, right?

segments.speaker: male
segments.text: 对的。还用刚才那个杯子的例子。如果外国来的杯子卖十块钱，而A国自己做的杯子要卖十五块钱。
segments.en: Right. Let's use the cup example again. If a foreign cup sells for ten yuan, but a cup made in Country A costs fifteen yuan.

segments.speaker: male
segments.text: 那么，大家肯定都去买外国的便宜杯子了。这样的话，A国做杯子的工厂可能就会关门。
segments.en: Then, everyone will definitely go buy the cheap foreign cup. If that happens, the cup factory in Country A might close down.

segments.speaker: female
segments.text: 哎，工厂关门了，那里的工人就会失去工作，没有钱生活了。
segments.en: Sigh, if the factory closes, the workers there will lose their jobs and have no money to live.

segments.speaker: male
segments.text: 是的。所以，如果把关税提高，外国的杯子也变成十五块钱，大家就会去买本国做的杯子了。
segments.en: Yes. So, if the tariff is raised and the foreign cup also becomes fifteen yuan, people will go buy the domestically made cup.

segments.speaker: female
segments.text: 原来是这样，政府是为了保护本地人的工作，这听起来也是可以理解的。
segments.en: So that's how it is. The government is trying to protect local people's jobs, which sounds understandable.

blocks.purpose: 从普通消费者的角度出发，聊聊关税带来的负面影响

segments.speaker: female
segments.text: 不过，如果我是那个国家的普通买家，我可能就不太开心了。
segments.en: However, if I were an ordinary buyer in that country, I might not be very happy.

segments.speaker: male
segments.text: 诶？为什么不开心呢？
segments.en: Eh? Why wouldn't you be happy?

segments.speaker: female
segments.text: 因为作为一个普通人，我只想要质量好、价格又便宜的杯子呀。我不太关心它是在哪里做的。
segments.en: Because as a normal person, I just want a cup that is of good quality and cheap. I don't really care where it's made.

segments.speaker: female
segments.text: 加了关税以后，便宜的杯子没有了，我只能花更多的钱去买东西，我的生活成本就变高了。
segments.en: After tariffs are added, there are no more cheap cups, and I have to spend more money to buy things, so my cost of living goes up.

segments.speaker: male
segments.text: 哈哈，盼盼，你说出了很多普通人的心里话。确实是这样。
segments.en: Haha, Panpan, you've spoken the inner thoughts of many ordinary people. It's indeed like this.

segments.speaker: male
segments.text: 关税是一把“双刃剑”，意思是有好处也有坏处。
segments.en: A tariff is a 'double-edged sword', meaning it has both pros and cons.

segments.speaker: male
segments.text: 一方面它保护了本地的公司，但另一方面，老百姓买东西就要花更多的钱。
segments.en: On one hand, it protects local companies, but on the other hand, ordinary people have to spend more money to buy things.

segments.speaker: female
segments.text: 是啊，生活中的每件事好像都是这样，很难让所有人都满意。
segments.en: Yeah, it seems everything in life is like this, it's hard to make everyone satisfied.

blocks.purpose: 话题转向中国，分析全球关税环境对中国出口的挑战

segments.speaker: male
segments.text: 那么，我们换个角度。大家知道，中国被称为“世界工厂”，有很多东西都是中国制造的。
segments.en: So, let's look at it from another angle. Everyone knows China is called the 'World's Factory', and many things are made in China.

segments.speaker: female
segments.text: 对。如果全世界都在增加关税，这对中国来说，肯定是一个非常大的挑战吧？
segments.en: Right. If the whole world is increasing tariffs, this must be a very huge challenge for China, right?

segments.speaker: male
segments.text: 影响当然非常大。以前，只要你的东西质量不错、价格便宜，就可以轻松卖到全世界。
segments.en: The impact is of course very big. Before, as long as your things were of good quality and cheap, you could easily sell them worldwide.

segments.speaker: male
segments.text: 但是现在，去很多国家的“门票”变贵了，这就逼着中国公司必须做出改变。
segments.en: But now, the 'tickets' to many countries have become expensive, which forces Chinese companies to make changes.

segments.speaker: female
segments.text: 是啊，如果门票太贵，东西卖不出去，中国的工厂也会遇到困难。
segments.en: Yeah, if the tickets are too expensive and things can't be sold, Chinese factories will also face difficulties.

segments.speaker: female
segments.text: 那他们会怎么应对呢？总不能坐在家里什么都不做吧？我觉得聪明的公司肯定有办法的。
segments.en: So how will they respond? They can't just sit at home doing nothing, right? I think smart companies definitely have a way.

segments.speaker: male
segments.text: 你说得很对。在这种全球都在变化的时代，中国公司其实主要有三个应对的方法。
segments.en: You are absolutely right. In this era of global change, Chinese companies actually have three main methods to respond.

segments.speaker: female
segments.text: 哇，三个方法？我已经迫不及待想听听了。路哥，第一个是什么？
segments.en: Wow, three methods? I can't wait to hear them. Brother Lu, what's the first one?

blocks.purpose: 讲解应对策略一：多元化出口，寻找新兴市场

segments.speaker: male
segments.text: 第一个办法，叫作“寻找新市场”。中文里有一句话叫“不要把鸡蛋放在同一个篮子里”。
segments.en: The first method is called 'finding new markets'. There is a saying in Chinese: 'Don't put all your eggs in one basket'.

segments.speaker: female
segments.text: 哈哈，这个比喻很有意思。意思是，万一这个篮子掉在地上，所有的鸡蛋就都碎了，对吗？
segments.en: Haha, this metaphor is very interesting. It means if this basket drops on the ground, all the eggs will break, right?

segments.speaker: male
segments.text: 完全正确。以前，很多公司可能只把东西卖给某几个大国家。现在他们发现这样风险太大了。
segments.en: Exactly right. Before, many companies might have only sold things to a few big countries. Now they find that this risk is too high.

segments.speaker: female
segments.text: 我明白了。如果这几个大国家突然加关税，生意就断了。
segments.en: I get it. If these few big countries suddenly increase tariffs, the business is cut off.

segments.speaker: male
segments.text: 是的。所以现在，很多公司开始去南美洲、中东、东南亚甚至非洲做生意。
segments.en: Yes. So now, many companies are starting to do business in South America, the Middle East, Southeast Asia, and even Africa.

segments.speaker: male
segments.text: 去认识更多的新朋友，把东西卖到更多不同的地方。
segments.en: To make more new friends and sell things to more different places.

segments.speaker: female
segments.text: 这样就算一个国家加关税，其他国家还可以继续合作，生意就不会死掉。
segments.en: This way, even if one country raises tariffs, they can still cooperate with other countries, and the business won't die.

segments.speaker: male
segments.text: 对。而且这样也能让世界上更多国家的人，用上中国制造的产品，大家互通有无。
segments.en: Right. And this also allows people in more countries around the world to use Chinese-made products, supplying each other's needs.

blocks.purpose: 讲解应对策略二：企业出海，在海外建厂，实现双赢

segments.speaker: female
segments.text: 那第二个方法是什么呢？
segments.en: Then what is the second method?

segments.speaker: male
segments.text: 第二个非常重要的方法，就是“走出去”，直接去别的国家建工厂。
segments.en: The second very important method is 'going out', building factories directly in other countries.

segments.speaker: female
segments.text: 哦！这个我知道。我有个朋友，原来在中国一家做汽车的公司上班。
segments.en: Oh! I know about this. I have a friend who used to work at a car company in China.

segments.speaker: female
segments.text: 去年他被派到了欧洲的一个国家去工作，因为他们公司在那里建了一个新工厂。
segments.en: Last year he was sent to work in a European country because their company built a new factory there.

segments.speaker: male
segments.text: 没错，这就是现在很流行的词，叫作“企业出海”。你觉得他们为什么要去国外建工厂呢？
segments.en: Exactly, this is a very popular phrase now, called 'enterprises going overseas'. Why do you think they go build factories abroad?

segments.speaker: female
segments.text: 我想想啊……如果在那个国家建了工厂，在那里生产汽车，再卖给当地人，那就不算“进口”了吧？
segments.en: Let me think... If they build a factory in that country, produce cars there, and sell them to locals, it wouldn't count as an 'import', right?

segments.speaker: female
segments.text: 既然不是从外国进来的，也就不用交很高的关税了。我说的对吗？
segments.en: Since it's not coming in from a foreign country, they wouldn't need to pay high tariffs. Am I right?

segments.speaker: male
segments.text: 非常聪明！就是这个道理。这样不仅解决了关税的问题，而且对当地国家也非常有好处。
segments.en: Very smart! That's exactly the logic. This not only solves the tariff problem but is also very beneficial to the local country.

segments.speaker: female
segments.text: 我猜到了！建工厂需要招当地的工人，这样当地人就有工作了，政府肯定也很欢迎。
segments.en: I guessed it! Building a factory requires hiring local workers, so locals have jobs, and the government must welcome it too.

segments.speaker: male
segments.text: 是的，这样就变成了一个“双赢”的局面，也就是对大家都有好处的合作。
segments.en: Yes, this turns into a 'win-win' situation, meaning cooperation that is beneficial to everyone.

blocks.purpose: 讲解应对策略三：提升科技和质量，打造不可替代性

segments.speaker: female
segments.text: 寻找新市场，还有去当地建工厂，这两个办法听起来都很棒。那第三个办法呢？
segments.en: Finding new markets and building local factories, both of these sound great. What about the third method?

segments.speaker: male
segments.text: 第三个办法需要更多的时间，但也最关键，那就是：提高产品的质量和技术。
segments.en: The third method takes more time but is the most crucial: improving product quality and technology.

segments.speaker: female
segments.text: 提高质量？这和关税有什么关系呢？如果是好东西，加了关税不是更贵了吗？
segments.en: Improving quality? What does that have to do with tariffs? If it's a good thing, doesn't it become even more expensive with tariffs?

segments.speaker: male
segments.text: 你想想看，如果你做的一件衣服很普通，到处都能买到。加了关税变贵了，大家就会去买别人的。
segments.en: Think about it, if a piece of clothing you make is very ordinary and can be bought anywhere. If it gets expensive due to tariffs, people will just buy someone else's.

segments.speaker: male
segments.text: 但是，如果你做的是一个非常棒的新手机，或者是非常先进的机器，别的地方买不到呢？
segments.en: But, what if what you make is a fantastic new phone, or a very advanced machine, that can't be bought elsewhere?

segments.speaker: female
segments.text: 哎，对啊！如果东西真的非常好，而且只有你有，就算加了关税变贵了，需要的人还是得买。
segments.en: Ah, that's right! If the thing is really great and only you have it, even if it gets expensive with tariffs, people who need it still have to buy it.

segments.speaker: male
segments.text: 就是这样。以前很多中国产品最大的优势是“便宜”。
segments.en: Exactly. Before, the biggest advantage of many Chinese products was being 'cheap'.

segments.speaker: male
segments.text: 但现在，关税的问题逼着大家必须花更多时间去研究新技术，做出更好的东西。
segments.en: But now, the tariff issue forces everyone to spend more time researching new technologies and making better things.

segments.speaker: female
segments.text: 这就叫“不可替代”。这么看的话，遇到困难反而成了一种动力，让大家变得更优秀了。
segments.en: This is called 'irreplaceable'. Looking at it this way, encountering difficulties has actually become a motivation, making everyone more excellent.

segments.speaker: male
segments.text: 没错。其实世界经济就是这样，总是会有各种各样的变化。聪明的公司总是能在变化中进步。
segments.en: Right. Actually, the global economy is just like this, there will always be various changes. Smart companies can always progress amid changes.

blocks.purpose: 总结整期内容，提问互动并呼吁订阅

segments.speaker: female
segments.text: 听你这么一说，我觉得今天学到了很多。以前看到关税的新闻觉得很头疼，现在清楚多了。
segments.en: Hearing you say this, I feel I've learned a lot today. I used to get a headache seeing news about tariffs, but it's much clearer now.

segments.speaker: male
segments.text: 那盼盼，你能给我们的听众朋友们简单总结一下我们今天聊的内容吗？
segments.en: So Panpan, can you briefly summarize what we talked about today for our listeners?

segments.speaker: female
segments.text: 没问题。面对全球增加关税的情况，中国的应对方法主要有三个：寻找新市场、去国外建工厂合作双赢，还有就是做出质量更好的东西。
segments.en: No problem. Facing the situation of global tariff increases, China's main response methods are three: finding new markets, building factories abroad for win-win cooperation, and making better quality products.

segments.speaker: male
segments.text: 总结得非常好。其实我们生活在同一个地球上，各个国家都是互相需要的，合作才能让大家都过得更好。
segments.en: Summarized very well. Actually, we live on the same Earth, countries all need each other, and only cooperation can make everyone's lives better.

segments.speaker: female
segments.text: 是的，作为普通人，我还是希望世界上的合作能多一点。这样大家都能买到好用又实惠的东西。
segments.en: Yes, as an ordinary person, I still hope for a bit more cooperation in the world. This way everyone can buy useful and affordable things.

segments.speaker: male
segments.text: 听众朋友们，在你们的国家，最近有讨论关税的新闻吗？或者你们国家的物价有什么变化吗？
segments.en: Listeners, has there been news discussing tariffs in your country recently? Or have there been any changes in prices in your country?

segments.speaker: female
segments.text: 欢迎大家在视频下面用中文留言，和我们分享你们那里的情况！用中文写评论是很好的练习哦。
segments.en: We welcome everyone to leave comments in Chinese below the video to share the situation in your area! Writing comments in Chinese is great practice.

segments.speaker: male
segments.text: 如果你喜欢我们的播客，觉得对你的中文听力有帮助，请给我们点赞、订阅，并分享给学习中文的朋友。
segments.en: If you like our podcast and feel it helps your Chinese listening, please like, subscribe, and share it with friends learning Chinese.

segments.speaker: female
segments.text: 感谢大家的陪伴。今天我们就聊到这里，我们下期再见啦！
segments.en: Thank you everyone for your company. We'll stop chatting here for today, see you next time!
